mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate over a 3-month window signals monetary easing that reduces the discount rate applied to equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.34% on 2016-01-01 indicates near-zero rates post-crisis
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or mean 20-day forward returns during easing periods do not exceed baseline by at least 1.5% (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Monthly payroll employment growth above 150,000 persons signals robust labor market expansion that sustains consumer income and spending, driving positive equity returns over 60 trading days as earnings expectations rise.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 143,288 thousand on 2016-01-01 reflects post-crisis recovery
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with sustained job creation
failure_condition: Triggered signals produce positive 60-day returns in fewer than 55% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month decline in retail sales (RSAFS) exceeding 2% signals demand weakness that compresses earnings expectations and equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as sentiment deteriorates.
failure_condition: Triggered signals produce negative 5-day returns in fewer than 50% of instances, or mean return during triggered periods is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth decelerates below 0.5% month-over-month while unemployment rises simultaneously, signaling stagflation risk that compresses equity risk premiums and produces negative 20-day forward returns as investors reduce cyclical exposure.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO MoM growth < 0.5% AND UNRATE rises > 0.2pp in 1 month
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 106.02 on 2015-12-01, UNRATE at 4.9% on 2016-01-01
failure_condition: Joint trigger produces negative 20-day returns in fewer than 52% of instances, or interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in logistic regression.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) accelerate above 1,200 thousand units annualized while real GDP growth remains positive, signaling sustained demand-side momentum that supports corporate earnings and equity valuations over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST > 1,200 thousand units AND GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter growth > 1.5%
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1,149 thousand on 2015-12-01, GDPC1 at 16,442.3 billion on 2015-10-01
regime_hint: post-crisis recovery with synchronized demand expansion
failure_condition: Joint trigger produces positive 60-day returns in fewer than 60% of instances, or Rank IC of interaction term < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) accelerates above 2.5% year-over-year while the federal funds rate remains below 1.5%, signaling real rate compression that erodes bond yields and redirects capital to equities, producing positive 20-day returns.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 237.847 on 2015-12-01, FEDFUNDS at 0.34% on 2016-01-01
regime_hint: low-rate inflation environment with financial repression
failure_condition: Joint trigger produces positive 20-day returns in fewer than 58% of instances, or the interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.15) in logistic regression.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate rises above 5.2% in a single month while payroll employment declines month-over-month, signaling labor market deterioration that triggers risk-off sentiment and negative equity returns over 5 trading days as forward earnings are revised downward.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 4.9% on 2016-01-01, PAYEMS at 143,288 thousand
regime_hint: labor market deterioration or early recession signal
failure_condition: Joint trigger produces negative 5-day returns in fewer than 50% of instances, or mean return during triggered periods is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.20).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production declines for two consecutive months while inflation remains subdued (CPIAUCSL YoY < 1.5%), signaling deflationary supply weakness that compresses profit margins and equity valuations, producing negative 60-day forward returns.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO month-over-month change < 0 for 2 consecutive months AND CPIAUCSL YoY growth < 1.5%
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 106.02 on 2015-12-01, CPIAUCSL at 237.847 on 2015-12-01
regime_hint: deflationary supply shock or demand-deficient recession
failure_condition: Triggered signals produce negative 60-day returns in fewer than 55% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.
